source_jobs.title: Create your Dream Job
source_jobs.description:
BranchLab is building the future of healthcare commercialization.

BranchLab is a healthcare AI company focused on predicting health outcomes to improving how regulated organizations make decisions.

Our mission is to enable better healthcare decisions by applying AI in a way that is privacy-safe, rigorous, and designed for real-world use.





INTERESTED IN JOINING BRANCHLAB?

Don’t see your perfect role listed but believe you can make an impact at BranchLab? We’d love to hear from you.

We’re always looking for talented, mission-driven individuals who share our passion for transforming healthcare marketing through responsible innovation and AI. Whether your expertise lies in data science, engineering, client strategy, marketing, product or operations there may be a future opportunity here that fits your skills and ambitions.

If you’re excited by our mission and see yourself contributing to a team that values transparency, excellence, collaboration, and purpose-driven technology, we invite you to apply.



In your application, tell us a bit about:

 - The type of role or department that interests you

 - How your background connects with BranchLab’s mission
   
   

We look forward to hearing from you!





BENEFITS

 - Competitive Salary & Comprehensive Benefits

 - Equity Opportunities – Grow with the company

 - Small, Agile Team – Your work will have a direct impact

 - A culture rooted in learning, collaboration, and respect

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Sales
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Enterprise Sales

Job Type: Full-time, Hybrid in office in NYC. Exceptional candidates outside the New York metropolitan area will also be considered.
Reports to: CRO
Work Authorization: Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the U.S. without the need for current or future sponsorship

ABOUT BRANCHLAB

 

BranchLab is an AI-native healthcare commercialization platform that helps pharmaceutical brands, healthcare marketers, agencies, and media partners identify, activate, and optimize audiences most likely to drive meaningful health outcomes.



Using predictive modeling and advanced audience intelligence, BranchLab enables clients to build custom patient, caregiver, and healthcare professional (HCP) audiences, activate them across digital, TV, social, audio, and out-of-home media, and optimize performance against real-world outcomes.



Our mission is to improve healthcare commercialization by helping organizations make faster and smarter marketing decisions while maintaining the highest standards of privacy, compliance, and scientific rigor.



We work with some of the world's leading healthcare organizations to improve how treatments, providers, and health information reach the people who need them most.



THE OPPORTUNITY



BranchLab is an AI-native healthcare commercialization platform that helps pharmaceutical brands, healthcare marketers, agencies, and media partners identify, activate, and optimize audiences most likely to drive meaningful health outcomes.

Using predictive modeling and advanced audience intelligence, BranchLab enables clients to build custom patient, caregiver, and healthcare professional (HCP) audiences, activate them across digital, TV, social, audio, and out-of-home media, and optimize performance against real-world outcomes.

Our mission is to improve healthcare commercialization by helping organizations make faster and smarter marketing decisions while maintaining the highest standards of privacy, compliance, and scientific rigor.

We work with some of the world's leading healthcare organizations to improve how treatments, providers, and health information reach the people who need them most.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and execute strategic sales plans to achieve and exceed revenue targets.

 - Identify, prospect, and cultivate relationships with pharmaceutical manufacturers, agencies, and other strategic accounts.

 - Lead discovery conversations to understand client objectives, challenges, and commercialization strategies.

 - Present BranchLab's platform, products, and services to prospective clients, tailoring solutions to specific business needs and use cases.

 - Build and manage a robust sales pipeline, maintaining accurate forecasting and opportunity tracking within CRM systems.

 - Develop proposals, pricing strategies, business cases, and commercial agreements in collaboration with internal stakeholders.

 - Own contract negotiations and drive opportunities through successful close.

 - Partner with Product, Data Science, Operations, and Client Success teams to ensure successful handoff, implementation, and long-term customer success.

 - Represent BranchLab at industry events, conferences, client meetings, and networking opportunities to increase market awareness and generate new business opportunities.

 - Gather market intelligence and customer feedback to inform product strategy, positioning, and go-to-market initiatives.

 - Drive account expansion opportunities by identifying additional use cases, products, channels, and services that deliver value to existing clients.

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

 - Bachelor's degree in related field.

 - Proven experience in enterprise sales, business development, strategic partnerships.

 - Track record of meeting or exceeding revenue targets in a B2B environment.

 - Excellent communication, presentation, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.

 - Strong consultative selling and solution-oriented prob

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Measurement, Planning & Optimization
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Senior Product Manager, Measurement, Planning & Optimization
Job Type: Full-time, Hybrid in office in Boulder, CO. We have an in-office culture, with a minimum 3 days in the office (Tues-Thurs).
Reporting To: Senior Director of Product
Work Authorization: Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the U.S. without the need for current or future sponsorship

ABOUT BRANCHLAB

BranchLab is an AI-native healthcare commercialization platform that helps pharmaceutical brands, healthcare marketers, agencies, and media partners identify, activate, and optimize audiences most likely to drive meaningful health outcomes.



Using predictive modeling and advanced audience intelligence, BranchLab enables clients to build custom patient, caregiver, and healthcare professional (HCP) audiences, activate them across digital, TV, social, audio, and out-of-home media, and optimize performance against real-world outcomes.



Our mission is to improve healthcare commercialization by helping organizations make faster and smarter marketing decisions while maintaining the highest standards of privacy, compliance, and scientific rigor.



We work with some of the world's leading healthcare organizations to improve how treatments, providers, and health information reach the people who need them most.



THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re hiring a Senior Product Manager to own our Measurement, Planning & Optimization product.

You’ll define how we:

 - Ingest and standardize fragmented measurement data

 - Connect media exposure to healthcare outcomes

 - Surface insights, KPIs, and recommendations

 - Enable smarter planning and budget optimization through artificial intelligence
   
   
   This is a 0→1, high-impact role at the core of our product strategy.
   

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build the measurement layer

Productize data ingestion, standardization, and reporting across multiple sources

 - Create a unified data model

Define a system that connects audiences, media, and outcomes

 - Define insights & recommendations

Establish KPIs that drive actionable recommendations

 - Shape an agent-first experience

Enable users to ask questions across media + commercial data and get insights with visuals

 - Lead cross-functionally

Partner with engineering, design, data, and GTM to bring the product to life



REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

 - 3-5+ years in product management, ideally in data-heavy or analytics products

 - High energy, high agency working style

 - Experience building and working with AI products and agentic workflows

 - Strong comfort with:

Data pipelines, messy datasets, and multi-source systems

Defining metrics, KPIs, and insight frameworks

 - Ability to operate in ambiguity and turn complexity into clear product direction

 - Demonstrated ability to operate effectively in fast-paced, early-stage environments with evolving priorities and limited structure

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in adtech, martech, or healthcare data

 - Familiarity with measurement platforms (e.g., CrossIX) or DSPs

 - Experience with AI/agent-driven UX
   

WHY JOIN BRANCHLAB?

 - Competitive Salary & Comprehensive Benefits

 - Small, Agile Team – Your work will have a direct impact

 - Equity Opportunities – Grow with the company

source_jobs.title: Software Development Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Employee Location: Boulder, CO, relocation stipend available
Reporting To: Chief Technology Officer
Job Type: Full-time, Hybrid in office Boulder, CO
Work Authorization: Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the U.S. without the need for current or future sponsorship



ABOUT BRANCHLAB

BranchLab is an AI-native healthcare commercialization platform that helps pharmaceutical brands, healthcare marketers, agencies, and media partners identify, activate, and optimize audiences most likely to drive meaningful health outcomes.



Using predictive modeling and advanced audience intelligence, BranchLab enables clients to build custom patient, caregiver, and healthcare professional (HCP) audiences, activate them across digital, TV, social, audio, and out-of-home media, and optimize performance against real-world outcomes.



Our mission is to improve healthcare commercialization by helping organizations make faster and smarter marketing decisions while maintaining the highest standards of privacy, compliance, and scientific rigor.



We work with some of the world's leading healthcare organizations to improve how treatments, providers, and health information reach the people who need them most.



THE OPPORTUNITY
We’re looking for a backend focused Sr. Software Engineer to join our Boulder-based team and help build the infrastructure powering our privacy first, data-driven platform. This role blends distributed systems engineering and cloud-native development in a high-impact environment. You’ll design scalable systems to process, transform, and query large volumes of anonymized healthcare data. You’ll also design systems to drive insights and power downstream applications.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Design, build, and maintain backend systems and APIs using Python.

 - Architect distributed systems using AWS services like EKS, EMR, S3 and Athena.

 - Deploy ML models in engineering pipelines.

 - Build and optimize ETL pipelines and secure data-sharing workflows using Spark.

 - Deploy and manage containerized services with Docker and Kubernetes, and manage infrastructure-as-code.

 - Work with large-scale datasets using advanced SQL for analytics, reporting, and data product features.

 - Ensure system reliability, scalability, and observability in a production environment.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Strong backend development experience in Python.

 - Familiarity with cloud-native architectures and services (ideally AWS).

 - Understanding of distributed systems and event-driven architectures.

 - Experience with containerization (Docker) and CI/CD pipelines.

 - Proficiency in writing SQL queries and working with large-scale datasets (Athena, Redshift, PostgreSQL, etc.).

 - A pragmatic problem-solving mindset with an eye for performance, security, and maintainability.



NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Familiarity with real-time data processing or streaming architectures.

 - Comfort with infrastructure-as-code tools (CloudFormation, CDK, etc.).

 - Background in data modeling, query optimization, and performance tuning.

 - Exposure to Healthcare, AdTech, or privacy-sensitive data systems.



WHY JOIN BRANCHLAB?

 - Competitive salary (145-195k) + significant equity

 - Tackle complex engineering challenges in privacy-first healthcare advertising.

 - Work alongside top-tier engineers and data scientists.

source_jobs.title: Sr. Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Sr. Product Designer
Job Type: Full-time, Hybrid in office in Boulder, CO. We have an in-office culture, with a minimum 3 days in the office (Tues-Thurs).
Reporting To: Senior Director of Product
Work Authorization: Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the U.S. without the need for current or future sponsorship

ABOUT BRANCHLAB

BranchLab is an AI-native healthcare commercialization platform that helps pharmaceutical brands, healthcare marketers, agencies, and media partners identify, activate, and optimize audiences most likely to drive meaningful health outcomes.



Using predictive modeling and advanced audience intelligence, BranchLab enables clients to build custom patient, caregiver, and healthcare professional (HCP) audiences, activate them across digital, TV, social, audio, and out-of-home media, and optimize performance against real-world outcomes.



Our mission is to improve healthcare commercialization by helping organizations make faster and smarter marketing decisions while maintaining the highest standards of privacy, compliance, and scientific rigor.



We work with some of the world's leading healthcare organizations to improve how treatments, providers, and health information reach the people who need them most.

THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for a Sr. Product Designer to own the end-to-end design process, from user research and problem definition to polished, production-ready UI.

You’ll play a key role in shaping both what we build and how it works, working closely with product, design, engineering, and clients. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced startup environment, enjoys ambiguity, and is equally comfortable talking to users, facilitating workshops, and crafting high-quality design.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


 - Own the end-to-end product design process: research → ideation → wireframes → high-fidelity UI → implementation

 - Lead user research efforts (interviews, usability testing, synthesis) to uncover insights and inform product decisions

 - Plan and facilitate design workshops with clients and stakeholders (discovery sessions, journey mapping, ideation)

 - Help define what we build and why, not just how it looks

 - Translate user needs and business goals into intuitive, elegant solutions

 - Rapidly prototype and iterate in a fast-moving environment

 - Collaborate closely with product and engineering to shape features and roadmap decisions

 - Design polished, production-ready UI with a strong attention to detail

 - Contribute to and evolve our design system

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

 - 5+ years of experience in Product Design, UI/UX, or similar role

 - Strong portfolio demonstrating end-to-end product thinking and research-driven design

 - Proven experience conducting user research and translating insights into product decisions

 - Experience facilitating or leading workshops with clients or cross-functional teams

 - Excellent UI/UX craft: interaction design, visual design, and usability

 - Ability to balance user needs, business goals, and technical constraints

 - Comfortable working directly with stakeholders and presenting ideas clearly

 - Ability to thrive in a small, fast-paced startup environment

 - High ownership mindset—you take ideas from concept to shipped product
   
   PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
   
   Familiarity with analytics and data-informed design

 - Experience building or scaling design systems

 - Background in service design or design thinking methodologies
   
   WHAT IT'S LIKE TO WORK HERE

 - High ownership: You’ll directly shape the product and user experience

 - Fast-paced: We ship quickly and iterate often

 - Small team: Minimal bureaucracy, maximum impact

 - Client exposure: You’ll work directly with users and stakeholders
   
   WHY JOIN BRANCHLAB?

 - Competitive Salary & Comprehensive Benefits

 - Small, Agile Team – Your work will have a direct impact

 - Equity Opportunities – Grow with the company

source_jobs.title: Brand & Marketing Designer
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Brand & Marketing Designer
Job Type: Full-time, Hybrid in office in NYC or Boulder, CO. We have an in-office culture, with a minimum 3 days in the office (Tues-Thurs).
Reporting to: Creative Director, UX & Brand

Partners with:VP of Marketing, Sales, Leadership

Focus Areas: Brand systems, marketing design, website, sales collateral, presentations, motion, AI assisted creative workflows
Work Authorization: Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the U.S. without the need for current or future sponsorship



ABOUT BRANCHLAB

BranchLab is an AI-native healthcare commercialization platform that helps pharmaceutical brands, healthcare marketers, agencies, and media partners identify, activate, and optimize audiences most likely to drive meaningful health outcomes.

 

Using predictive modeling and advanced audience intelligence, BranchLab enables clients to build custom patient, caregiver, and healthcare professional (HCP) audiences, activate them across digital, TV, social, audio, and out-of-home media, and optimize performance against real-world outcomes.

 

Our mission is to improve healthcare commercialization by helping organizations make faster and smarter marketing decisions while maintaining the highest standards of privacy, compliance, and scientific rigor.

 

We work with some of the world's leading healthcare organizations to improve how treatments, providers, and health information reach the people who need them most.



THE OPPORTUNITY



BranchLab is looking for a Brand & Marketing Designer to help build, scale, and evolve the visual identity of the company across brand, marketing, sales, and client experience.

This person will work closely with the Creative Director, UX & Brand, the VP of Marketing, Sales, Product, and leadership to create high-quality visual materials that support BranchLab’s growth, market presence, and personalized client experience strategy.

This role is ideal for a highly creative, collaborative, and organized designer who can move quickly, think conceptually, and execute with polish. The right person enjoys bringing new ideas to the table, exploring multiple design directions, and seeing their work make a direct impact on brand perception, marketing campaigns, sales conversations, and client engagement.

The Brand & Marketing Designer will support both traditional marketing design needs and the development of BranchLab DNA, our personalized client experience system for priority accounts. This includes scalable brand guidelines, presentation systems, sales materials, event assets, motion graphics, website support, and account-specific visual storytelling.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




BRAND & IDENTITY SYSTEM

· Help expand and maintain the BranchLab visual identity across marketing, sales, digital, and client-facing materials.

· Support the development of scalable brand guidelines, including typography, color, imagery, iconography, layout, motion, and visual storytelling principles.

· Create design systems and reusable assets that allow the company to move faster while maintaining consistency and quality.

· Ensure all materials feel polished, thoughtful, differentiated, and aligned with BranchLab’s brand standards.

· Bring fresh creative ideas and visual concepts that help set BranchLab apart in the market.




MARKETING DESIGN

· Design marketing assets across campaigns, social media, events, digital channels, and internal initiatives.

· Partner with the VP of Marketing to support ongoing marketing priorities, campaign needs, and growth initiatives.

· Create display ads, campaign graphics, website visuals, landing page assets, event materials, and other marketing deliverables.

· Translate marketing strategy into clear, compelling, and visually engaging creative.

· Develop materials that support demand generation, brand awareness, pipeline, and revenue growth.




SALES & CLIENT-FACING MATERIALS

· Design high-quality sales collateral, including pitch decks, one

source_jobs.title: Client Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Client Success Manager
Job Type: Full-time, Hybrid in office in NYC.
Reporting To: CRO
Work Authorization: Applicants must have legal authorization to work in the U.S. without the need for current or future sponsorship



ABOUT BRANCHLAB

BranchLab is an AI-native healthcare commercialization platform that helps pharmaceutical brands, healthcare marketers, agencies, and media partners identify, activate, and optimize audiences most likely to drive meaningful health outcomes.



Using predictive modeling and advanced audience intelligence, BranchLab enables clients to build custom patient, caregiver, and healthcare professional (HCP) audiences, activate them across digital, TV, social, audio, and out-of-home media, and optimize performance against real-world outcomes.



Our mission is to improve healthcare commercialization by helping organizations make faster and smarter marketing decisions while maintaining the highest standards of privacy, compliance, and scientific rigor.



We work with some of the world's leading healthcare organizations to improve how treatments, providers, and health information reach the people who need them most.



THE OPPORTUNITY

We're looking for a proactive, client-focused professional who thrives in a fast-paced environment and enjoys solving complex business challenges. The ideal candidate combines strong relationship management skills with analytical thinking and a passion for helping clients achieve measurable results.

As a Client Success Manager, you will partner with leading pharmaceutical manufacturers, agencies, and media partners to help them maximize value from BranchLab's platform and services.

You will support clients via managed-service and as self-service users of platform solutions, helping organizations successfully onboard, adopt, and scale their use of our technology.

You will work across our core solutions, including:

 - Custom Patient (DTC), Caregiver, HCP, and Consumer Audience Development

 - Audience Activation across Programmatic, TV, Social, Audio, and Out-of-Home Media

 - Audience Performance Measurement and Optimization

 - Outcome-Based Reporting and Analytics

 - Strategic Audience Planning and Research

 - Self-Service Audience Design, Activation, and Analytics Workflows

You will serve as a trusted advisor to clients, helping them understand how BranchLab's predictive audience technology can improve campaign performance, accelerate activation, and support business growth. Whether supporting enterprise managed-service engagements or driving adoption of our self-service platform, you will play a critical role in ensuring clients achieve measurable business outcomes and realize the full value of their partnership with BranchLab.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own relationships with pharmaceutical manufacturers, healthcare agencies, and media partners, serving as a strategic advisor throughout the client lifecycle.

 - Lead onboarding and implementation for new clients, ensuring successful adoption of BranchLab solutions and alignment on business objectives.

 - Partner with clients to define audience strategies, activation plans, success metrics, and measurement approaches.

 - Deliver regular business reviews that demonstrate campaign performance, audience insights, and return on investment.

 - Collaborate with Product, Data Science, Operations, and Sales teams to ensure successful execution and continuous client value creation.

 - Identify opportunities to expand client engagement through additional audience solutions, activation channels, analytics capabilities, and offerings.

 - Gather client feedback to inform product roadmap priorities and improve the overall customer experience.

 - Monitor adoption, utilization, retention, and satisfaction metrics while proactively addressing risks and opportunities.
   
   
   
   REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

 - Bachelor’s degree in related field.

 - Proven experience in client-facing roles, such as account ma

notes: AI-native healthcare commercialization platform with a mix of tech and non-tech roles, but a strong focus on product development.
